open_hooks_to_carry: Resist false catharsis; the ending should hold injury, survival, and dark humor at once.
Show what official language leaves out of the year they lived.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...the fall. The bill.

June moved to stand beside her. Close enough that Leela could smell coffee and stale mint from the gum.

"Don't thank me," June said.

"I wasn't going to."

"Good."

Another beat passed.

"You can later," June said.

Leela laughed then, unexpectedly and without elegance, the sound scraping on the way out. It hurt a little. It also helped.

Omar gathered his folder, already preparing to go upstairs to Gloria before the office corridor filled with faculty advising one another how shocked to sound. Ben stayed where he was, staring at the lab as if an adult might still emerge from the server closet and explain the next semester to him. June tore the used top sheet off her legal pad and folded it smaller, once, then again, until it disappeared into her fist.

Around them the Civic Signals Lab remained stubbornly itself for a few more minutes: the dead marker smell, the old upholstery, the server heat breathing out of the closet, the ugly little ecosystem that had fed them and used them both. Then the red light on the badge reader at Mohit's office blinked, the preservation hold finalized, and the room changed in a way that had nothing to do with furniture.

They had stopped him. That part was true.

The rest of it was true too: the summer funding that might vanish, the papers stranded mid-sentence, the recommendation network gone radioactive, the degree timelines already broken, the simple fact that there was no clean institution waiting behind the dirty man. The lab had finally stopped belonging to him by becoming a place that did not belong to any of them.
state_deltas_required: Leela and the others must decide what remains of their work, their friendships, and their professional futures after Mohit's fall.

Leela submits a stripped-down dissertation under a provisional arrangement and begins a one-year teaching fellowship, while the former lab keeps the encrypted archive as the private residue of a broken collective.
